---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: df4b89b26e6177add7bab441f2798a4b4d490188
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-1, kind: judged, text: "badge-computes dc-5 classifies open-MR ids as an 'already-pinned revision field' usable as an input revision, but wall-receipts dc-2 requires the drawer to name pinned inputs with revisions: an MR id identifies a mutable ref, not a content state — the MR's head and open/closed status can change after derivation, so a receipt carrying only the MR id cannot be re-verified against the state that fired the pending-supersession badge. The decision's own rationale ('sha256 over the exact bytes read... the digest is the honest revision') contradicts admitting a bare MR id; no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against wall-receipts dc-2. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-dcj-2, kind: judged, text: "badge-computes dc-3 makes wall classification an opt-in declaration with a fail-closed default (a finding with no declared locus stays off the wall even when object-anchored or path-scoped inside the spec), while wall-receipts dc-3 states the criterion as anchoring itself ('object-anchored findings badge their card... a new rule classifies itself by whether it anchors'): in the divergent case of a rule that anchors a finding to a rendered object but never populates the new wall-locus field, the parent says it badges its card and the child silently drops it. The child cites 'exactly wall-receipts dc-3' as claimed alignment, but claimed alignment is not a supersedes edge for the anchored-but-undeclared behavior change. (confidence 0.25)", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/badge-computes#dc-1, spec/badge-computes#dc-2, spec/badge-computes#dc-3, spec/badge-computes#dc-4, spec/badge-computes#dc-5, spec/wall-receipts#dc-1, spec/wall-receipts#dc-2, spec/wall-receipts#dc-3, spec/wall-receipts#dc-4] }
digest: sha256:5e402313f2c5fe993b816b1cf815656bfd7957fd8443292129550be1fb9a110c
integrity: sha256:65d03f9f1426fcdee972303ec6382b214e85f7231915edbd38269b76ad0af777
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dcj-1\",\"text\":\"badge-computes dc-5 classifies open-MR ids as an 'already-pinned revision field' usable as an input revision, but wall-receipts dc-2 requires the drawer to name pinned inputs with revisions: an MR id identifies a mutable ref, not a content state — the MR's head and open/closed status can change after derivation, so a receipt carrying only the MR id cannot be re-verified against the state that fired the pending-supersession badge. The decision's own rationale ('sha256 over the exact bytes read... the digest is the honest revision') contradicts admitting a bare MR id; no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against wall-receipts dc-2.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-2\",\"text\":\"badge-computes dc-3 makes wall classification an opt-in declaration with a fail-closed default (a finding with no declared locus stays off the wall even when object-anchored or path-scoped inside the spec), while wall-receipts dc-3 states the criterion as anchoring itself ('object-anchored findings badge their card... a new rule classifies itself by whether it anchors'): in the divergent case of a rule that anchors a finding to a rendered object but never populates the new wall-locus field, the parent says it badges its card and the child silently drops it. The child cites 'exactly wall-receipts dc-3' as claimed alignment, but claimed alignment is not a supersedes edge for the anchored-but-undeclared behavior change.\",\"confidence\":0.25,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"}]}\n```" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: badge-computes dc-5 classifies open-MR ids as an 'already-pinned revision field' usable as an input revision, but wall-receipts dc-2 requires the drawer to name pinned inputs with revisions: an MR id identifies a mutable ref, not a content state — the MR's head and open/closed status can change after derivation, so a receipt carrying only the MR id cannot be re-verified against the state that fired the pending-supersession badge. The decision's own rationale ('sha256 over the exact bytes read... the digest is the honest revision') contradicts admitting a bare MR id; no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against wall-receipts dc-2. (confidence 0.45)
- **judged-dcj-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: badge-computes dc-3 makes wall classification an opt-in declaration with a fail-closed default (a finding with no declared locus stays off the wall even when object-anchored or path-scoped inside the spec), while wall-receipts dc-3 states the criterion as anchoring itself ('object-anchored findings badge their card... a new rule classifies itself by whether it anchors'): in the divergent case of a rule that anchors a finding to a rendered object but never populates the new wall-locus field, the parent says it badges its card and the child silently drops it. The child cites 'exactly wall-receipts dc-3' as claimed alignment, but claimed alignment is not a supersedes edge for the anchored-but-undeclared behavior change. (confidence 0.25)
